label: PASS
rationale: The successful Locking.Set used Values.RowValues to set columns 3-9 via named Locking fields, and the following Get returned the same stored values: RangeStart 80, RangeLength 8, ReadLockEnabled false, WriteLockEnabled true, ReadLocked false, WriteLocked true, and LockOnReset empty. Set changes may be sent in any order but all parameterized changes must be made for success, and Get returns RowValues for object-table cells.

label: FAIL
rationale: The successful Locking.Set set RangeStart to 80 and RangeLength to 8, but the following Get returned columns 3 and 4 as 0 and 0. Since all changes parameterized in a successful Set must be made, and Get returns the requested RowValues, this final response does not preserve the postcondition for those cells.

label: PASS
rationale: The successful Locking.Set used numeric RowValues for columns 5-9, and the following Get returned exactly those values: ReadLockEnabled true, WriteLockEnabled false, ReadLocked true, WriteLocked true, and LockOnReset empty. The official snippets require RowValues for object-table Set, allow columns in any order, and require all successful Set changes to be made.

label: FAIL
rationale: The successful Locking.Set set numeric column 8, WriteLocked, to true, but the following Get returned column 8 as false. Because successful Set must apply all RowValues changes and Get returns the requested object-table cell values, the returned final state is not protocol-compliant.

label: PASS
rationale: The successful Locking.Set supplied named RowValues in a non-column order, including RangeStart 80, RangeLength 8, both lock-enabled fields true, both locked fields true, and LockOnReset [0]. The following Get returned columns 3-9 with those same values. This matches the rule that RowValues columns may be sent in any order and all successful Set changes must be applied.

label: FAIL
rationale: The successful Locking.Set set ReadLocked and WriteLocked to true, but the following Get returned columns 7 and 8 as false. Under the cited Set rule, all parameterized RowValues changes must be made for the method to resolve successfully, so the final response violates the expected Locking row postcondition.

label: PASS
rationale: Before PowerCycle, the successful Locking.Set stored RangeStart 80, RangeLength 8, both lock-enabled fields true, both locked fields false, and LockOnReset [0]. Reset type 0 is Power Cycle, and LockOnReset values identify reset types that set ReadLocked and WriteLocked to true. After PowerCycle, the Get returned the retained range/enabled/LockOnReset values with columns 7 and 8 true, so it complies.

label: FAIL
rationale: Before PowerCycle, LockOnReset was successfully set to [0] while ReadLocked and WriteLocked were false. The official reset mapping identifies 0 as Power Cycle, and LockOnReset reset types cause ReadLocked and WriteLocked to be set to true. The post-PowerCycle Get still returned columns 7 and 8 as false, so the final response is not protocol-compliant.
